title: Watching a language model think before it speaks
excerpt: Subtext hooks a local 4B model's residual stream and projects it into vocabulary space, so you can watch the words show up in its head a beat before they show up in its mouth.
content_html:
Projects

A 4B model, a lens on its residual stream, and a browser tab where the words show up in its head a beat before they show up in its mouth.

I typed "Is this correct? 12 + 5 = 1" and hit enter, and before the model wrote a single character of a reply, three words were already sitting in a little panel off to the side: math, addition, incorrect. It hadn't said anything yet. It was still reading my message. But it had clearly already made up its mind, and I was watching that happen, live, one layer at a time.

I've been playing with this for about a week and I still get a small jolt every time. So I cleaned it up and put it on GitHub. It's called Subtext, and the whole idea is very simple to state: instead of only reading what a model says, you get to read what it's about to say, at every step, while it reads and while it writes.

What it actually shows you

Normally you interact with a model through the one channel it's optimized to make look good: its output. Subtext taps the channel underneath. It hooks the residual stream at nine layers, and at every token it takes those internal activations and projects them into vocabulary space, so you get a ranked list of the words the model is leaning toward internally. Not the word it emits. The words it's disposed to emit. You watch those readouts stream past as it reads your prompt and as it generates its answer.

The part that surprised me is how much is going on before the first output token. There's a whole "reading" phase where the model is just ingesting your message, and the workspace is already busy: categories, judgments, the gist of where this is going. By the time it starts typing, a lot of the interesting decisions look like they've already been made.

How it works, roughly

The trick underneath is an old interpretability idea with a sharper new version. The old idea is the logit lens: take the hidden state at some middle layer, pretend it's the final layer, run it through the model's unembedding, and read off the top words. It sort of works. Each layer is holding a fuzzy running guess about what comes next, and if you squint at it through the output matrix you can read the guess.

The sharper version Subtext uses is the Jacobian lens, from a recent interpretability paper. Roughly: instead of naively decoding the raw hidden state, it isolates the slice of the representation that the model can actually verbally report and reuse downstream (the paper calls it a global workspace, a "J-space"), and decodes that. In practice it's one small matrix-vector product per layer plus an unembedding per token, which is cheap enough that generation still streams at basically native speed. You're not waiting on the introspection. It just runs alongside.

Nine layers means you don't get one answer, you get a little cross-section of the network. You can watch a concept show up shallow and vague and then sharpen as it climbs.

Three things I didn't expect

It settles the verdict before it says a word. That 12+5 example wasn't a fluke. Over and over, the workspace holds incorrect at high strength while the model is still politely typing "Let me take a look at that." The conclusion is locked in several tokens before the sentence that delivers it. Watching a model be diplomatic on the surface while it's already decided underneath is a weird feeling.

It plans ahead of its own mouth. Mid-explanation, the technical term it's about to reach for is frequently already parked in the workspace, a few tokens early, waiting its turn. The output reads left to right. The thinking clearly doesn't.

You can watch it hop. This is my favorite. There's a known "two-hop" behavior where a model answers "what's the currency of the boot-shaped country" by internally going boot-shaped → Italy → euro. In Subtext you can actually see both feet land: Italy shows up around layer 20, euros shows up around layer 26, and this is all before it generates anything. It's one of those things you've read about in papers and it hits differently when it's scrolling by on your own screen.

The caveats, which you should actually read

I want to be careful here, because this is exactly the kind of demo that's easy to over-read, and I'd rather you trust the honest version.

It's a lens, which means it's an approximation. I checked it against the reference implementation and the top-5 readouts match with cosine similarity above 0.99998, so the decode itself is faithful. But a faithful decode of a projection is still a projection. These words are what a linear readout says the model is disposed toward. Calling them the model's "thoughts" is a metaphor, and a generous one.

It's also a 4B model. Small. And the display is filtered down to word-initial tokens so it's legible, so you're seeing a cleaned-up view, not the raw vocabulary. Most importantly: this is correlation. The lens can light up incorrect without that reading being the thing that causes the output. It is a beautiful instrument. It is not a lie detector, and I'd push back on anyone who sells it as one.

Why I think this matters anyway

Even with all that, something shifted for me. Reading a model has always meant reading the surface it was trained to polish. This is a look at the layer under the paint, streaming, at native speed, on a GPU I already own. My honest guess is that "just watch the model think" becomes a normal move: for debugging a prompt, for catching a model that's confidently wrong before it commits, for teaching people what these things are actually doing instead of hand-waving at a black box. For an afternoon it stopped feeling like a wall and started feeling like a window.

And the replay part makes it shareable, which I didn't expect to care about but do. Every session exports to JSON with all the lens frames, and the frontend can replay the whole thing in a browser with no GPU at all. So you can capture a weird moment and hand it to someone and they can scrub through it on a laptop.

Running it

It's a local thing. You need roughly 10GB of VRAM on an NVIDIA card, or an Apple Silicon Mac with 16GB or more. It's Python plus a single HTML file that talks to the server over a websocket. First launch pulls the model and the pre-fitted lens (about 9GB) and builds a token mask, which takes a minute; after that it's instant.

  1. Clone it: git clone https://github.com/ninjahawk/Subtext
  2. Enter the directory: cd Subtext
  3. Install the deps: pip install -r requirements.txt
  4. Start the server: python server.py
  5. Open http://localhost:8765

Then ask it something you already know is wrong, and watch incorrect light up before it's polite enough to tell you. It's the most fun I've had staring at a residual stream.

Repo: github.com/ninjahawk/Subtext. It's Apache-2.0, and it's an independent project, not affiliated with Anthropic.

sentences.zh: 这些词是线性读数表示模型倾向于的内容。

sentences.en: Calling them the model's "thoughts" is a metaphor, and a generous one.
sentences.zh: 将它们称为模型的“想法”是一种隐喻，而且是一个慷慨的隐喻。

blocks.html_en: It's also a 4B model. Small. And the display is filtered down to word-initial tokens so it's legible, so you're seeing a cleaned-up view, not the raw vocabulary. Most importantly: this is correlation. The lens can light up incorrect without that reading being the thing that causes the output. It is a beautiful instrument. It is not a lie detector, and I'd push back on anyone who sells it as one.

sentences.en: It's also a 4B model.
sentences.zh: 这也是4B型号。

sentences.en: Small.
sentences.zh: 小的。

sentences.en: And the display is filtered down to word-initial tokens so it's legible, so you're seeing a cleaned-up view, not the raw vocabulary.
sentences.zh: 显示内容被过滤为词首标记，因此清晰易读，因此您看到的是经过清理的视图，而不是原始词汇表。

sentences.en: Most importantly: this is correlation.
sentences.zh: 最重要的是：这是相关性。

sentences.en: The lens can light up incorrect without that reading being the thing that causes the output.
sentences.zh: 如果读数不是导致输出的原因，则镜头可能会错误地点亮。

sentences.en: It is a beautiful instrument.
sentences.zh: 这是一种美丽的乐器。

sentences.en: It is not a lie detector, and I'd push back on anyone who sells it as one.
sentences.zh: 它不是测谎仪，我会拒绝任何将其作为测谎仪出售的人。

blocks.html_en: Why I think this matters anyway

sentences.en: Why I think this matters anyway
sentences.zh: 为什么我认为这很重要

blocks.html_en: Even with all that, something shifted for me. Reading a model has always meant reading the surface it was trained to polish. This is a look at the layer under the paint, streaming, at native speed, on a GPU I already own. My honest guess is that "just watch the model think" becomes a normal move: for debugging a prompt, for catching a model that's confidently wrong before it commits, for teaching people what these things are actually doing instead of hand-waving at a black box. For an afternoon it stopped feeling like a wall and started feeling like a window.

sentences.en: Even with all that, something shifted for me.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，有些事情对我来说发生了变化。

sentences.en: Reading a model has always meant reading the surface it was trained to polish.
sentences.zh: 阅读模型始终意味着阅读经过训练的抛光表面。

sentences.en: This is a look at the layer under the paint, streaming, at native speed, on a GPU I already own.
sentences.zh: 这是在我已经拥有的 GPU 上以本机速度流式传输的绘制下层的视图。

sentences.en: My honest guess is that "just watch the model think" becomes a normal move: for debugging a prompt, for catching a model that's confidently wrong before it commits, for teaching people what these things are actually doing instead of hand-waving at a black box.
sentences.zh: 我诚实的猜测是，“只是观察模型思考”成为一个正常的举动：用于调试提示，用于在提交之前捕获一个确信错误的模型，用于教导人们这些东西实际上在做什么，而不是在黑匣子上挥手。

sentences.en: For an afternoon it stopped feeling like a wall and started feeling like a window.
sentences.zh: 一个下午，它不再感觉像一堵墙，而开始感觉像一扇窗户。

blocks.html_en: And the replay part makes it shareable, which I didn't expect to care about but do. Every session exports to JSON with all the lens frames, and the frontend can replay the whole thing in a browser with no GPU at all. So you can capture a weird moment and hand it to someone and they can scrub through it on a laptop.

sentences.en: And the replay part makes it shareable, which I didn't expect to care about but do.
sentences.zh: 重播部分使其可以共享，我原本没想到会关心这一点，但确实如此。

sentences.en: Every session exports to JSON with all the lens frames, and the frontend can replay the whole thing in a browser with no GPU at all.
sentences.zh: 每个会话都将所有镜头帧导出为 JSON，前端可以在浏览器中重播整个内容，而无需 GPU。

sentences.en: So you can capture a weird moment and hand it to someone and they can scrub through it on a laptop.
sentences.zh: 因此，您可以捕捉一个奇怪的时刻并将其交给某人，他们可以在笔记本电脑上进行清理。

blocks.html_en: Running it

sentences.en: Running it
sentences.zh: 运行它

blocks.html_en: It's a local thing. You need roughly 10GB of VRAM on an NVIDIA card, or an Apple Silicon Mac with 16GB or more. It's Python plus a single HTML file that talks to the server over a websocket. First launch pulls the model and the pre-fitted lens (about 9GB) and builds a token mask, which takes a minute; after that it's instant.

sentences.en: It's a local thing.
sentences.zh: 这是当地的事情。

sentences.en: You need roughly 10GB of VRAM on an NVIDIA card, or an Apple Silicon Mac with 16GB or more.
sentences.zh: 您需要 NVIDIA 卡上大约 10GB 的 VRAM，或者具有 16GB 或更多的 Apple Silicon Mac。

sentences.en: It's Python plus a single HTML file that talks to the server over a websocket.
sentences.zh: 它是 Python 加上一个 HTML 文件，通过 websocket 与服务器通信。

sentences.en: First launch pulls the model and the pre-fitted lens (about 9GB) and builds a token mask, which takes a minute; after that it's instant.
sentences.zh: 首次启动会拉动模型和预装镜头（约 9GB）并构建令牌掩模，这需要一分钟；之后就是即时的。

blocks.html_en: • Clone it: git clone https://github.com/ninjahawk/Subtext

sentences.en: Clone it: git clone https://github.com/ninjahawk/Subtext
sentences.zh: 克隆它： git clone https://github.com/ninjahawk/Subtext

blocks.html_en: • Enter the directory: cd Subtext

sentences.en: Enter the directory: cd Subtext
sentences.zh: 进入目录：cd 潜台词

blocks.html_en: • Install the deps: pip install -r requirements.txt

sentences.en: Install the deps: pip install -r requirements.txt
sentences.zh: 安装依赖项： pip install -rrequirements.txt

blocks.html_en: • Start the server: python server.py

sentences.en: Start the server: python server.py
sentences.zh: 启动服务器：python server.py

blocks.html_en: • Open http://localhost:8765

sentences.en: Open http://localhost:8765
sentences.zh: 打开http://localhost:8765

blocks.html_en: Then ask it something you already know is wrong, and watch incorrect light up before it's polite enough to tell you. It's the most fun I've had staring at a residual stream.

sentences.en: Then ask it something you already know is wrong, and watch incorrect light up before it's polite enough to tell you.
sentences.zh: 然后问它一些你已经知道是错误的事情，并在它足够礼貌地告诉你之前看到错误的灯亮起。

sentences.en: It's the most fun I've had staring at a residual stream.
sentences.zh: 这是我盯着残流看的最有趣的事情。

blocks.html_en: Repo: github.com/ninjahawk/Subtext. It's Apache-2.0, and it's an independent project, not affiliated with Anthropic.

sentences.en: Repo: github.com/ninjahawk/Subtext.
sentences.zh: 仓库：github.com/ninjahawk/Subtext。

sentences.en: It's Apache-2.0, and it's an independent project, not affiliated with Anthropic.
sentences.zh: 它是 Apache-2.0，它是一个独立的项目，不隶属于 Anthropic。

content_html_zh:
项目

一个 4B 模型，在其剩余流上有一个镜头，还有一个浏览器选项卡，其中的文字在出现在其嘴里之前会先在其头部显示一次。

我输入“这是正确的吗？ 12 + 5 = 1”并按回车键，在模型写出回复的单个字符之前，三个单词已经坐在旁边的小面板中：数学、加法、不正确。 它还没有说什么。 它仍在读我的消息。 但它显然已经下定决心，而我正在现场观看这一切的发生，一次一层。

我已经玩了大约一周了，每次仍然会感到轻微的震动。 所以我把它清理了一下，放到了 GitHub 上。 它被称为潜文本，整个想法很容易表述：您不仅可以阅读模型所说的内容，还可以在每一步读取和写入时阅读它要说的内容。

它实际上向您展示了什么

通常，您通过模型经过优化以使其看起来不错的一个通道与模型进行交互：它的输出。 潜台词点击下方的通道。 它将残差流挂在九层，并且在每个标记处，它都会获取这些内部激活并将它们投影到词汇空间中，因此您可以获得模型内部倾向于的单词的排名列表。 不是它发出的词。 它准备发出的话语。 当它读取您的提示并生成答案时，您会看到这些读数流过。

让我惊讶的部分是在第一个输出令牌之前发生了多少事情。 有一个完整的“阅读”阶段，模型只是摄取您的消息，而工作区已经很忙：类别、判断、要点。 当它开始输入时，许多有趣的决定看起来已经做出了。

大致是如何运作的

其背后的技巧是一个旧的可解释性想法与一个更清晰的新版本。 旧的想法是逻辑透镜：获取某个中间层的隐藏状态，假装它是最后一层，通过模型的非嵌入运行它，并读出最上面的单词。 这有点管用。 每层都对接下来发生的事情进行模糊的运行猜测，如果你通过输出矩阵观察它，你可以读取猜测。

Subtext 使用的更清晰的版本是雅可比透镜，来自最近的一篇可解释性论文。 粗略地说：它不是简单地解码原始隐藏状态，而是隔离模型实际上可以口头报告并在下游重用的表示切片（本文将其称为全局工作区，“J-空间”），并对其进行解码。 实际上，它是每层一个小的矩阵向量乘积加上每个令牌的非嵌入，这足够便宜，以至于生成仍然以基本本机速度进行流式传输。 你不是在等待内省。 它只是并肩运行。

九层意味着你不会得到一个答案，你会得到网络的一小部分横截面。 你可以看到一个概念出现的浅薄和模糊，然后随着它的上升而变得尖锐。

三件事是我没想到的

它在说出一句话之前就已经做出了裁决。 那个 12+5 的例子并不是侥幸。 一次又一次，工作空间在高强度下保持不正确，而模型仍然礼貌地输入“让我看一下”。 结论被锁定在给出结论的句子之前的几个标记中。 看着一个模型表面上外交得体，但内心已经决定是一种奇怪的感觉。

它提前计划好自己的嘴。 在解释过程中，它即将触及的技术术语通常已经停在工作空间中，提前几个令牌，等待轮到它。 输出从左到右读取。 想法显然不是。

你可以看着它跳跃。 这是我最喜欢的。 有一种已知的“两跳”行为，模型通过内部靴子形状 → 意大利 → 欧元来回答“靴子形状国家的货币是什么”。 在 Subtext 中，你实际上可以看到双脚落地：意大利出现在第 20 层附近，欧元出现在第 26 层附近，这都是在它生成任何东西之前。 这是你在报纸上读到的内容之一，当它在你自己的屏幕上滚动时，它的冲击力会有所不同。

您应该实际阅读的注意事项

我在这里要小心，因为这正是那种容易被过度阅读的演示，我宁愿你相信诚实的版本。

这是一个镜头，这意味着它是一个近似值。 我根据参考实现对其进行了检查，前 5 个读数与高于 0.99998 的余弦相似度匹配，因此解码本身是忠实的。 但投影的忠实解码仍然是投影。 这些词是线性读数表示模型倾向于的内容。 将它们称为模型的“想法”是一种隐喻，而且是一个慷慨的隐喻。

这也是4B型号。 小的。 显示内容被过滤为词首标记，因此清晰易读，因此您看到的是经过清理的视图，而不是原始词汇表。 最重要的是：这是相关性。 如果读数不是导致输出的原因，则镜头可能会错误地点亮。 这是一种美丽的乐器。 它不是测谎仪，我会拒绝任何将其作为测谎仪出售的人。

为什么我认为这很重要

尽管如此，有些事情对我来说发生了变化。 阅读模型始终意味着阅读经过训练的抛光表面。 这是在我已经拥有的 GPU 上以本机速度流式传输的绘制下层的视图。 我诚实的猜测是，“只是观察模型思考”成为一个正常的举动：用于调试提示，用于在提交之前捕获一个确信错误的模型，用于教导人们这些东西实际上在做什么，而不是在黑匣子上挥手。 一个下午，它不再感觉像一堵墙，而开始感觉像一扇窗户。

重播部分使其可以共享，我原本没想到会关心这一点，但确实如此。 每个会话都将所有镜头帧导出为 JSON，前端可以在浏览器中重播整个内容，而无需 GPU。 因此，您可以捕捉一个奇怪的时刻并将其交给某人，他们可以在笔记本电脑上进行清理。

运行它

这是当地的事情。 您需要 NVIDIA 卡上大约 10GB 的 VRAM，或者具有 16GB 或更多的 Apple Silicon Mac。 它是 Python 加上一个 HTML 文件，通过 websocket 与服务器通信。 首次启动会拉动模型和预装镜头（约 9GB）并构建令牌掩模，这需要一分钟；之后就是即时的。

  1. 克隆它： git clone https://github.com/ninjahawk/Subtext
  2. 进入目录：cd 潜台词
  3. 安装依赖项： pip install -rrequirements.txt
  4. 启动服务器：python server.py
  5. 打开http://localhost:8765

然后问它一些你已经知道是错误的事情，并在它足够礼貌地告诉你之前看到错误的灯亮起。 这是我盯着残流看的最有趣的事情。

仓库：github.com/ninjahawk/Subtext。 它是 Apache-2.0，它是一个独立的项目，不隶属于 Anthropic。
